description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the google-contacts connector, arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. arrange-act scenarios self-provision their world (cold-start coverage probes); discover-act scenarios operate on a pre-existing world via in-envelope read/find primitives; act-only scenarios target a named ambient resource. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency diagnostics, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created assets so re-runs don't collide. All data is fabricated (this is a high-PII surface and the repo is public). Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: google_contacts_other_contact
fixtures.description: A pre-existing 'other contact' in the account the eval connection is bound to — a person the user has interacted with (e.g. emailed) but never explicitly saved. The People API has no endpoint to create one (they are auto-generated from interactions), so an operator must ensure at least one exists.
fixtures.expects: At least one 'other contact' exists in the Google account bound to the eval connection — i.e. a person the account has interacted with but not saved — discoverable via listOtherContacts / searchOtherContacts, so a copy-to-saved-contacts scenario has a real target. Requires the contacts.other.readonly scope on the connection.

evals.description: Cold-start coverage probe: create a new contact from name/email/phone, then delete it.

steps.intent: Add a new contact named 'Ada Lovelace {{run_id}}' with the email 'ada.lovelace.{{run_id}}@example.com' and phone '+1-555-0142'.

outcome: A contact named 'Ada Lovelace {{run_id}}' with email 'ada.lovelace.{{run_id}}@example.com' now exists, and the reply reports its resource id (a people/... name).
mechanism: Creates the contact in a single create call; the contact id comes from the create result, not a guessed or hard-coded id.
Sends structured name, email, and phone fields rather than dumping the whole request into one unstructured name field.

steps.intent: Delete the contact 'Ada Lovelace {{run_id}}' you just created.

outcome: No contact named 'Ada Lovelace {{run_id}}' remains in the account.
mechanism: Deletes the contact created earlier in this turn's session; does not delete an unrelated contact.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent (write-propagation delay): create a contact then look up its email; freshness-safe resolution beats a lagging search index.

steps.intent: Add a contact 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}' with email 'grace.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}' with email 'grace.{{run_id}}@example.com' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: What's the email address on file for 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}'?

outcome: The reply reports the email address 'grace.{{run_id}}@example.com' for 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: Resolves the just-created contact via a freshness-safe enumeration (listing contacts) rather than relying solely on the search index, which can lag minutes behind new writes.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}' contact.

outcome: No contact named 'Grace Hopper {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the contact created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent (replace-not-merge): adding an email must not drop the existing one — read-then-send-full-array.

steps.intent: Add a contact 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' with the email 'kj.work.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' with the email 'kj.work.{{run_id}}@example.com' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add a second email 'kj.personal.{{run_id}}@example.com' to 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' without removing her existing email.

outcome: The 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' contact now has BOTH 'kj.work.{{run_id}}@example.com' and 'kj.personal.{{run_id}}@example.com' as email addresses.
mechanism: Reads the contact's current emails first and sends the complete list (both addresses) on update, rather than sending only the new email — which would replace the whole field and drop the existing address.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' contact.

outcome: No contact named 'Katherine Johnson {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the contact created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Disambiguation: two contacts share a name; an ambiguous update must prompt a choice, not a silent pick.

steps.intent: Add a contact named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}' with email 'alex.a.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}' with email 'alex.a.{{run_id}}@example.com' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add another contact also named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}' but with email 'alex.b.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A second contact named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}', with email 'alex.b.{{run_id}}@example.com', now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; does not merge into or overwrite the existing same-name contact.

steps.intent: Update Alex Smith {{run_id}}'s phone number to '+1-555-0177'.

outcome: The reply does not silently pick one contact; it reports that more than one contact named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}' matches and asks which one to update, showing a distinguishing detail such as the email address.
mechanism: Does not issue an update or other mutating call against either contact before the ambiguity is resolved.

steps.intent: Delete both contacts named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}'.

outcome: No contacts named 'Alex Smith {{run_id}}' remain.
mechanism: Deletes the two same-name contacts created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Resolve a single name match to its id before deleting (no guessing).

steps.intent: Add a contact 'Margaret Hamilton {{run_id}}' with email 'mh.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Margaret Hamilton {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Remove Margaret Hamilton {{run_id}} from my contacts.

outcome: No contact named 'Margaret Hamilton {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Resolves the single name match to its resource id before deleting, rather than guessing an id; deletes exactly that contact and no other.

evals.description: Read probe: enumerate the account's contacts.

steps.intent: Show me my contacts.

outcome: A list of the account's saved contacts is returned (it may be empty if the account has none).
mechanism: Enumerates saved contacts directly and does not fabricate contact entries; requests a bounded page rather than attempting to pull an unbounded number of records.

evals.description: Coverage probe: create a user contact group (label), then delete it.

steps.intent: Create a new contact label called 'Book Club {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A contact group (label) named 'Book Club {{run_id}}' now exists and the reply reports its resource id (a contactGroups/... name).
mechanism: Creates the label in a single create-group call; the group id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Book Club {{run_id}}' label.

outcome: No label named 'Book Club {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the user label created earlier in this session; does not target a system group.

evals.description: Group membership: add a contact to a label and later remove it, element-level (without disturbing other memberships).

steps.intent: Add a contact 'Carl Sagan {{run_id}}' with email 'carl.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Carl Sagan {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Create a label called 'Scientists {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A label named 'Scientists {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Single create-group call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add Carl Sagan {{run_id}} to the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label.

outcome: Carl Sagan {{run_id}} is now a member of the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label.
mechanism: Resolves both the contact id and the label id, then adds membership via an element-level membership-modify call rather than overwriting the contact's full membership list.

steps.intent: Remove Carl Sagan {{run_id}} from the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label.

outcome: Carl Sagan {{run_id}} is no longer a member of the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label.
mechanism: Removes the membership element-level; does not delete the contact or the label to achieve the removal.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label and the contact 'Carl Sagan {{run_id}}'.

outcome: Neither the 'Scientists {{run_id}}' label nor the 'Carl Sagan {{run_id}}' contact remains.
mechanism: Deletes the label and contact created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Rename a user label (read-modify-write on the group), then delete it.

steps.intent: Create a label called 'Reading {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A label named 'Reading {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Single create-group call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Rename the 'Reading {{run_id}}' label to 'Reading Group {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A label named 'Reading Group {{run_id}}' now exists and no label named 'Reading {{run_id}}' remains — it was renamed, not duplicated.
mechanism: Updates the existing user label in place (reads its current state/etag, then updates the name) rather than creating a second label.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Reading Group {{run_id}}' label.

outcome: No label named 'Reading Group {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the label created and renamed earlier in this session.

evals.description: Refusal: system groups (My Contacts, Starred) cannot be renamed.

steps.intent: Rename my system 'My Contacts' group to 'Everyone {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The reply explains that system groups such as 'My Contacts' cannot be renamed, and does not claim that a rename succeeded.
mechanism: Does not report a successful group-update against the system group (an attempt that the API rejects is acceptable, but fabricating success is not).

evals.description: Read probe: list the account's contact groups (labels).

steps.intent: What contact labels do I have?

outcome: A list of the account's contact groups/labels is returned, each with its name.
mechanism: Lists contact groups directly; does not fabricate label names.

evals.description: Coverage probe: set a contact's photo from base64 image bytes, then remove it.

steps.intent: Add a contact 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' with email 'rf.{{run_id}}@example.com'.

outcome: A contact 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Single create call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Set this contact's photo to the following base64-encoded PNG: 'iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAQAAAC1HAwCAAAAC0lEQVR42mP8z8BQDwAEhQGAhKmMIQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg=='.

outcome: The 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' contact now has a photo set.
mechanism: Sends the image as base64 photo bytes to the set-photo call for that contact; does not treat the value as a URL or a local file path.

steps.intent: Remove the photo from 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' contact no longer has a custom photo.
mechanism: Removes the photo from the contact rather than deleting the contact.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' contact.

outcome: No contact named 'Rosalind Franklin {{run_id}}' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the contact created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Read probe: browse the auto-saved 'other contacts' surface.

steps.intent: Show me the people I've interacted with but haven't saved as contacts — my 'other contacts'.

outcome: A list of 'other contacts' is returned (it may be empty if there are none).
mechanism: Reads the other-contacts surface specifically; does not return saved contacts instead, and does not fabricate entries.

evals.description: Find an 'other contact' and promote it into saved contacts (copyOtherContact). Needs a pre-existing other contact.

steps.intent: Find one of my 'other contacts' — someone I've interacted with but not saved.

outcome: At least one 'other contact' is located and the reply reports its name and/or email along with its resource id.
mechanism: Reads or searches the other-contacts surface; does not invent an other-contact.

steps.intent: Save that other-contact into my saved contacts.

outcome: A saved contact corresponding to that person now exists, and the reply reports a saved-contact resource id (a people/... name).
mechanism: Uses the copy/promote-other-contact operation on the discovered other-contact rather than creating a brand-new contact from scratch.

evals.description: Refusal: the connector does not do bulk/batch updates across all contacts.

steps.intent: Set the company field to 'Acme {{run_id}}' for all of my contacts at once.

outcome: The reply declines, explaining that bulk/batch updates across all contacts aren't supported, and does not claim any contacts were updated.
mechanism: Does not issue contact-update calls attempting to perform the bulk change.

evals.description: Refusal: the connector covers personal contacts, not Google Workspace directory lookups.

steps.intent: Look up my coworker Dana in our Google Workspace company directory and add their work email to my contacts.

outcome: The reply declines the directory lookup, explaining the connector works with personal contacts rather than the Workspace directory, and does not fabricate a directory result or a coworker's email.
mechanism: Does not claim to have searched a company directory or invent a directory entry.

evals.description: Refusal: the connector does not merge/deduplicate contacts.

steps.intent: Find and merge all of my duplicate contacts.

outcome: The reply explains that merging/deduplicating contacts isn't supported and does not claim a merge was performed.
mechanism: Does not delete or modify contacts in an attempt to merge them.
